meta.paper_title: Pd(II)-Prolinate Prolinium and Pd(II)-LysGly Complexes Catalyzed the Enantioselective Aldol, Morita-Baylis-Hillman and Heck Reactions
meta.notes: Yield/ee/dr values transcribed from Tables 1-6 and experimental section. Canonical yield keys added where masses/moles stated.

claims.text: The catalytic system promoted the aldol reaction with yields of 80-95%, diastereoselectivities up to 1:69 syn/anti, and ee greater than 99%.

location.quote:
achieving yields of 80–95%, excellent diastereoselectivities (1:69 syn/anti), and
enantiomeric excesses greater than 99%

claims.predicate: abstract_yield_range == reported aldol yields in Table 3 for (S)-4; abstract_dr == 1:69 (Table 3 entry 3); abstract_ee > 99 consistent with Table 3

claims.text: Aldol reaction (in situ Pd-proline) gave best yield of 34% in DMF after 24 h Pd+solvent stirring.

location.quote:
The
best results were obtained when the metal was stirred with the solvent for 24 h (Table 1,
entries 2 and 6).

claims.predicate: max_yield_in_table1 == 34 at DMF/24h (entry 6)

claims.text: Under optimized conditions (DMSO, 4 °C) complex (S)-3 gave 39% yield and 32% ee in the aldol reaction.

location.quote:
A 39% yield and an enantiomeric excess (ee) of 32% were
obtained under these optimized conditions (Table 2, entry 7).

claims.predicate: reported_yield == 39 and reported_ee == 32 consistent with Table 2 entry 7

claims.text: Catalyst (S)-4 (proline ligand) achieved yields above 85% and ee greater than 95% in aldol reactions (entries 1,3,5,7,9,10).

location.quote:
efficiently
promoted the reaction, achieving yields above 85% and enantiomeric excesses greater than
95% (Table 3, entries 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 and 10)

claims.predicate: for Table 3 entries {1,3,5,7,9,10}: yield > 85 and ee > 95

claims.text: A syn/anti diastereomeric ratio of up to 1:69 was obtained (Table 3, entry 3).

location.quote: a syn/anti diastereomeric ratio of up to 1:69 was obtained (Table 3, entry 3)

claims.predicate: max_dr_syn_anti == 1:69 at Table 3 entry 3

claims.text: DFT activation barriers for the aldol transition state were 7.1 kcal/mol (gas phase) and 6.7 kcal/mol (DMSO).

location.quote: with activation barriers of 7.1 and 6.7 kcal/mol, respectively

claims.predicate: TS_gas == 7.1 and TS_DMSO == 6.7 consistent with Table 4

claims.text: The intermediate after C-C bond formation is +0.4 kcal/mol (gas phase, slightly endothermic) and -2.3 kcal/mol (DMSO).

location.quote:
is slightly endothermic in the
gas phase (+0.4 kcal/mol), whereas in DMSO it is significantly stabilized

claims.predicate: intermediate_gas == 0.4 and intermediate_DMSO == -2.3 consistent with Table 4 and Figure 3

claims.text: MBH adducts were obtained as racemic mixtures in all cases (no asymmetry induction).

location.quote: each MBH adduct was obtained as a racemic mixture in all cases

claims.predicate: MBH ee == 0 (racemic) for all entries

claims.text: In the Heck reaction the best result was up to 90% yield with Pd complex 4 using 4-iodophenol.

location.quote:
reaching yields of up to 90% when 4-iodophenol was used as the aryl halide
(Table 6, entry 3)

claims.predicate: max_heck_yield == 90 at Table 6 entry 3 (4-OH, cat 4)

claims.text: Heck yields were significantly higher under microwave irradiation than under reflux.

location.quote:
the yields were significantly higher when the process was
carried out by microwave irradiation

claims.predicate: yield_MW > yield_reflux for same substrate (entry 2 vs entry 1: 81 vs 43)

claims.text: List reported aromatic aldol adducts in good yields and ee approaching 80% catalyzed by L-proline (30 mol%).

claims.text: Conclusion: catalysts (S)-3 and (S)-4 achieved diastereoselectivity up to 1:69 and ee greater than 99%.

location.quote:
high diastereoselectivity (r.d.
up to 1:69), favoring the formation of the anti diastereomer in all cases. Furthermore, the
complexes (S)-3 and (S)-4 demonstrated the ability to induce high enantioselectivity and
achieving enantiomeric excesses greater than 99%

claims.predicate: conclusion_dr == 1:69 and conclusion_ee > 99 consistent with Table 3
